name: Waratah Super Battery
notable: Australia's largest battery at 850 MW / 1,680 MWh. Core of the NSW System Integrity Protection Scheme (SIPS) enabling higher transmission utilisation after Eraring coal closure. Total AER-approved SIPS revenue $705.2M over 5 years (battery + paired generators + Transgrid network). Suffered catastrophic transformer failure Oct 2025 with estimated $50-80M revenue loss. Funded by Akaysha Energy (BlackRock) with $100M CEFC investment. Tesla Megapack 2XL.

timeline.title: Akaysha Energy selected for NSW SIPS battery
timeline.detail: Akaysha (BlackRock) selected by NSW govt to build 850MW/1,680MWh battery at Eraring site. AER total revenue determination $705.2M over 5yr (Jul 2024-Jun 2029): SIPS battery service >$600M (contestable) + ~$104M network augmentation/control system (Transgrid).

timeline.title: Construction begins
timeline.detail: Powin selected as battery OEM (2,592 Centipede Energy Segments), EKS Energy for 288 power conversion systems, CPP as EPC, Wilson Transformer for three 350MVA transformers.

timeline.title: Commercial operations commenced
timeline.detail: Commencement date from AEMO registration via OpenElectricity.

timeline.title: First generation recorded
timeline.detail: First market dispatch data recorded in AEMO systems.

timeline.title: Battery OEM Powin files Chapter 11 bankruptcy
timeline.detail: Powin filed for Chapter 11 in US Bankruptcy Court with >M debt. Causes include tariffs on Chinese imports and IRA uncertainty. Assets sold to FlexGen Aug 2025. Long-term warranty implications for Waratah.

timeline.title: Partial COD at 350MW (41% capacity)
timeline.detail: First 350MW/700MWh commenced operations under SIPS obligations using one of three transformers (HVT1). Battery registered as NEM largest single DUID.

timeline.title: Catastrophic transformer failure (HVT3)
timeline.detail: Wilson Transformer HVT3 suffered catastrophic internal fault 4 hours before completing final Hold Point 5 testing. Overpressure event ruptured tank wall. Transformer beyond repair. HVT2 also de-energised as precaution. Battery capped at 350MW. Estimated loss AU-80M.

timeline.title: Full plant shutdown for transformer remediation
timeline.detail: Full balance-of-plant shutdown 20 Nov to 2 Dec 2025 for HVT2 inspection and remediation works.

timeline.title: Replacement transformer manufacturing underway
timeline.detail: Wilson Transformer manufacturing replacement HVT3. Design review completed by CPP and independent consultants. Delivery to site targeted Q3 2026.

timeline.title: Full 850MW operations targeted end of 2026
timeline.detail: All three transformers operational targeted for end of 2026 pending HVT3 replacement delivery (Q3 2026) and HVT2 remediation completion.

scheme_contracts.contract_type: SIPS total revenue $705.2M/5yr (Jul 2024-Jun 2029): battery service >$600M + network augmentation ~$104M

stakeholder_issues.detail: Major transformer failure caused extended outage, triggering SIPS availability payment reductions. Estimated revenue loss $50-80M. Significant media scrutiny from AFR, RenewEconomy, WattClarity. AER approved revenue adjustment March 2025.

eis_specs.document_title: Waratah Super Battery — State Significant Development Assessment (NSW IPC SSD-2022-0195)
eis_specs.connection_substation_name: Tomago 330 kV substation (TransGrid), Hunter Valley NSW
eis_specs.connection_distance_note: ~2 km from BESS site to TransGrid Tomago 330 kV substation, Hunter Valley NSW.
eis_specs.connection_augmentation: TransGrid Tomago substation upgrade for 850 MW BESS. NSW IPC SSD assessment included detailed TransGrid connection feasibility. Critical grid support as Hunter Valley coal plants retire.
eis_specs.notes: 850 MW / 1,680 MWh (2-hour) BESS, Tomago, Hunter Valley NSW. Developer: Akaysha Energy (BlackRock Real Assets). Technology: Powin Centipede Energy Segment (LFP, CATL). Inverter: EKS Energy (Hitachi Energy) PCS. EPC: CPP. NSW IPC SSD-2022-0195. Now operating (2025). One of the largest grid-scale batteries in the world. TransGrid NSP confirmed in project data.

sources.title: AER — Waratah Super Battery Total Revenue
AER — Network Augmentation and SIPS Control System
RenewEconomy — Giant Waratah Super Battery to receive more than $700m
RenewEconomy — Waratah Super Battery revenue haircut

timeline_events.title: NSW Minister directs Transgrid to carry out SIPS project
timeline_events.detail: Classified as Priority Transmission Infrastructure Project (PTIP) under the Electricity Infrastructure Investment (EII) Act. EnergyCo selected Akaysha Energy as SIPS battery service provider.

timeline_events.title: NSW Government announces Akaysha Energy as tender winner
timeline_events.detail: Akaysha Energy (backed by BlackRock) selected to build 850 MW / 1,680 MWh battery at Eraring site for SIPS services

timeline_events.title: AER issues SIPS battery service revenue determination
timeline_events.detail: AER determines the revenue Transgrid may recover for the SIPS battery service component. Contestable amount (battery + paired generators combined) is commercially confidential.

timeline_events.title: AER determines paired generation services revenue
timeline_events.detail: Revenue determination for paired generators that rapidly reduce output during SIPS contingency events. Third provider added May 2024.

timeline_events.title: AER approves Transgrid network augmentation revenue (~$105M over 5 years)
timeline_events.detail: Non-contestable component: ~$104.2M (nominal) for network augmentations (line raising, tightening, substation upgrades) and SIPS control and communications system. Revenue cap ~$106.8M for 2024-29 regulatory period.

sources.title: AER — Network Augmentation and SIPS Control System

timeline_events.title: AER publishes total WSB revenue: $705.2M nominal over 5 years
timeline_events.detail: Aggregated total revenue across all 3 components (SIPS battery, paired generators, network/control). Breakdown: ~$105M Transgrid network, ~$600M+ contestable (battery + paired generators — split redacted as commercially sensitive). Payments flow: NSW consumers → distributors → Scheme Financial Vehicle → Transgrid → service providers.

sources.title: AER — Waratah Super Battery Total Revenue
RenewEconomy — Giant Waratah Super Battery to receive more than $700m

timeline_events.title: Full commercial operations — 850 MW / 1,680 MWh
timeline_events.detail: Commissioned as Australia's largest battery and one of the largest in the world. Delivered ahead of original schedule.

timeline_events.title: Catastrophic transformer failure takes battery offline
timeline_events.detail: Major transformer failure resulted in extended outage. Estimated revenue loss $50-80M from SIPS availability payments. Significant media scrutiny of project reliability.

sources.title: RenewEconomy — Waratah Super Battery revenue haircut

timeline_events.title: Transgrid submits revenue adjustment proposal to AER
timeline_events.detail: Revenue adjustment due to commissioning delays and transformer failure. AER approved a reduction to SIPS battery service payments — amounts redacted as commercially sensitive.
